name: Golden Apricot Ginger Cake (Low Fat)
recipeInstructions:
1. Note: To make ginger juice, place a piece of ginger root (about 2 or 3 inches) in a food processor. Puree, then wrap in cheesecloth and squeeze out the juice.
2. Melt butter into a 10-inch round glass tart or flan baking dish. Set aside.
3. Blend together cream cheese, crystallized ginger, half teaspoon sugar, and quarter teaspoon vanilla extract until smooth.
4. Spoon the cream cheese mixture into the centers of the apricot halves. Set aside.
5. Whisk together non-fat milk, apricot puree, thawed egg substitute, and ginger juice until well combined.
6. Stir together flour, baking powder, and baking soda in a separate bowl.
7. Add the apricot mixture to the flour mixture, stirring just blended.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared baking dish.
9. Push the filled apricots onto the cake batter at random places.
10. Sprinkle with gingersnap cookies.
11. Bake at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for about 20 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center tests clean.
12. Serve warm with additional apricot puree if desired.
For those interested, each serving contains: 179 calories, 103 mg sodium, 8 mg cholesterol, 3 grams fat, 36 grams carbohydrates, 4 grams protein, and 0.27 gram fiber.
